title: alchemilla arvensis f. pusilla
intro: Alchemilla arvensis f. pusilla, also known as dwarf lady's mantle, is a small flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla arvensis f. pusilla have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
